titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the James Payne Alter Papers
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Martha Lund Smalley and Brad Pohlman

controlnote.p: This encoded findingaid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

conventiondeclaration.abbr: finding aid prepared according to local divinity library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday September 2, 2020 at 22:36

did.abstract: The papers document Alter's lifelong involvement with the Christian church in India, his affiliation with Yale University, his activities in student Christian organizations, and his ongoing interest in issues of peace and social concern. James Payne Alter was born in India in 1919, son of Presbyterian missionaries. He attended Yale College and Yale Divinity School. He was appointed as a Presbyterian missionary to India in 1945. He taught at Ewing Christian College in Allahabad, served as first Director of the ecumenical Christian Retreat and Study Center, Rajpur, and wrote on Christianity in northern India.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

chronitemset.event: Born in Sialkot, Punjab, India to David Emmett Alter and Martha Payne Alter; his parents were missionaries of the former United Presbyterian Church of North America.
Attended Woodstock School, Mussourie, India
Attended Mount Hermon School, Massachusetts
Attended Yale University, B.A. 1940
Secretary of Yale University Christian Association
Three month prison term at Danbury Correctional Center as Conscientious Objector
Attended Yale Divinity School
Married Barbara Beach, daughter of David Nelson Beach II. (Children: Martha (1944), John (1947), and Thomas (1950))
Student Pastor, Haydenburg, Tennessee, Cumberland Mountain Presbytery under the auspices of the Presbyterian Board of National Missions
Continued studies at Yale Divinity School
Assigned to the mission field in India by the Board of Foreign Missions of the Presbyterian Church in U.S.A. Arrived in India October, 1945
Attended Leonard Theological College, Jabalpur, India
Professor of English, Chaplain, and Chair of the Department of Religion and Ethics, Ewing Christian College, Allahabad, India
Ordained by the Allahabad Church Council of the United Church of North India
Continued studies at Yale Divinity School, B.D. 1952
Served as Study Secretary for the World Council of Churches
First Director of ecumenical Christian Retreat and Study Centre, Rajpur, India
Researched, traveled, and published articles relating to the history of North Indian Christianity
Left India on medical retirement
Died
In the Doab and Rohilkhand : North Indian Christianity 1815-1915 (Delhi : ISPCK, 1986). Published posthumously, revised and completed by John Alter, son of James Payne Alter

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: James Payne Alter Papers (RG 84), Special Collections, Yale Divinity School Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Place names were modernized in the description, with the name originally used in the collection material or in an older version of the finding aid in parenthesis: e.g. "Beijing (Peking)" or "Benin (Dahomey)".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This collection documents James Alter's lifelong involvement with the Christian church in India, his affiliation with Yale University, his activities in student Christian organizations, and his ongoing interest in issues of peace and social concern.

Alter's student years at Yale, his involvement in the interrelated student Christian groups of that era, and his experiences as a conscientious objector during the Second World War are well-represented. After his return to India, extensive correspondence, writings and collected material provide important information about people and events as well as providing insight into Alter's personal journey as a son of traditional Presbyterian missionaries returning to devote his own life to an evolving participation in and understanding of the Christian Church in northern India. This record group does not contain a great deal of material relating specifically to the Christian Retreat and Study Centre in Rajpur, which Alter directed from 1954 to 1968. His consulting activities and research after this period are well documented.

The Correspondence of Series I, dating from 1936 to 1983, is divided into Family Correspondence and General Correspondence. Correspondence with family members, particularly his parents, is frequent and substantive. These letters, combined with the circular letters prepared by Jim and Barry Alter for their family and friends, provide thorough documentation of their activities for four decades. A fair portion of the General Correspondence is with the Presbyterian mission board and Presbyterian churches throughout the U.S. that provided financial support for the Alters. Also included in the General Correspondence section are letters documenting Alter's involvement in student Christian work, peace groups, and ecumenical ventures.

Series II is comprised of writings by Alter, including unpublished manuscripts, student papers, book reviews, sermons, lectures, and published works. The majority of the unpublished manuscripts and drafts contained in this Series relate to Christianity in northern India. Many are also available in published form, but drafts have been retained to document changes and revisions.

The materials contained in Series III and Series IV are similar in many ways, but Series III, Research Materials, represents the files that Alter had intentionally gathered together to support his ongoing research. These research materials relate almost exclusively to Christianity in northern India or to the interaction between Christianity and Communism. They include articles, reports, letters, notes, and newspaper clippings.

The Collected Material of Series IV is similar in format but covers a broader range of subject matter. Some materials relate to Christianity in India while others relate to courses taken by Alter, peace groups such as the Fellowship of Reconciliation, student Christian groups, and other organizations with which Alter was affiliated throughout his life, including the United Presbyterian Church Commission on Ecumenical Mission and Relations and the World Council of Churches.

Series V, Biographical Material, gathers together documentation relating to trips, projects, and other events in Alter's life. The Series includes notes, reports, itineraries, photographs, and official documents.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Letters are grouped in two sections, family correspondence and general correspondence, and are arranged alphabetically by sender or recipient other than Alter. Frequent or notable correspondents are listed separately while less substantial correspondence is grouped under the first letter of the sender or recipient's surname.

did.unittitle: Alter, David Emmett and Martha Payne [parents]

Family correspondence to James Payne Alter while in prison at Danbury Federal Correctional Institution, Danbury Connecticut [includes limited correspondence with fellow inmates]

Presbyterian Church in U.S.A. - Board of Foreign Missions

Rolston, Right Rev. M. A. Zarine, Diocese of Chandigarh
Socialist Groups - includes : Socialist Party, U.S.A., Young Peoples Socialist League
Student Christian Movement of New England
Student Christian Movement of New England
Student Christian Movement of New England : correspondence to friend, "Johny"
Swift, Charles R. and Mary Lou
United Presbyterian Church in the U.S.A.
Correspondence to Alters in India, including correspondence related to their India property
Webster, John C. [see Series V. Biographical Material]
West Charlton United Presbyterian Church, Amsterdam, NY
Wiggin and Dana : Counsellors at Law
American Presbyterians in British India - drafts
"Bible and Christian Social Action", in National Christian Council Review (1953) - reprint copy
Book on Devotions - includes letter by Ursula Niebuhr (Mrs. Reinhold Niebuhr)
"Christian Study and Lay Training Centers" report published by Commission on Ecumenical Mission and Relations, United Presbyterian Church in the U.S.A. - published copy
Christianity and British Rule in North India - draft
"Christianity, British Rule and the Challenge of Indian Nationalism", in Reflection (Christian Retreat and Study Centre, 1971/3), draft and published copy
Christianity and Communism - topical collected manuscripts
"Christianity in Uttar Pradesh : Prospectus of a study", in Reflection (Christian Retreat and Study Centre, 1975) - reprint copy
Edwin Wallace Parker and the Beginnings of Indian Methodism
India and Pakistan : Issues behind Conflict and Indian and Pakistan at War
Interview with Indonesia - draft and outline
In the Doab and Rohilkhand - drafts and notes of James Payne Alter and son John Alter
"India Attempts Democracy", in Christianity and Crisis 9:13 (1951), drafts and published copy
"Liberty, Equality, Fraternity", in Indian Church History Review 8:1 (1974), reprint copy
"Presbyterians in Farrukhabad", in Indian Church History Review 9:2 and 10:1 (1975-1976), reprint copy
Presbyterians in Farrukhabad - later revisions by John Alter. Published by John Alter as "Half-way house: Presbyterians in Farrukhabad, 1838-1915" in Journal of Presbyterian History 62:3 (1984)
Some observations on the Yale Senior Societies
Strategy and tactics of Indian communism - drafts
World Council of Churches meets at Evanston
Analysis of Composite Situations - paper for Presbyterian polity course
Contribution of Mysticism to Ethics - paper for H. Richard Niebuhr (includes comments from Niebuhr)
Platonic and Prophetic Conceptions of God - paper for Robert L. Calhoun (includes comments from Calhoun)
Anglican Church in India by M. E. Gibbs. Published review in Indian Church History Review 7:2 (1973)
Caesar the beloved enemy by M. A. C. Warren
Indian Working Class by Dr. Mukerjee
Paternalism and the Church by Michael Hallis
Christian Community and change in nineteenth century North India by John C. B. Webster. Published review in Indian Church History Review 10:2 (1976)
"Christian Institute for the Study of Society" - bulletin
Church Faces the Challenge report by Church of Scotland Commission on Communism
"Common Christian Responsibility" Issued by Department on Church and Society, World Council of Churches
Lecture - Christian ministers and the study of history
Autobiographies, includes - Prakash Masih, Ganga Nath Shukul, Solomon Nihal Singh, and Zuhur-ul-Haqq
"Evangelistic work in town and country", by C. H. Bundy
"Forty Years in the C.M.S. by a convert from Hinduism" by Rev. Canon Solomon Nihal Singh
Glance backward at fifteen years of missionary life in North India by Joseph Warren
Looking Back by Dr. J. J. Lucas
Memoirs of R. S. Fullerton and family. Includes editorial note by James Payne Alter and memoirs of Mrs. Leonard Waldo
Memorial of the Futtehgurh Mission by J. J. Walsh
"National Missionary Society of India" by Donald Fossett Ebright
North India Mission of Presbyterian Church - letters
North India Mission of Presbyterian Church - survey reports
North India Mission of Presbyterian Church - reports
"Prayer" by J. H. Nichols and Radha Swami Sect by H. D. Griswold

title.part: Report of the North India Conference of Christian workers

did.unittitle: "Theory and practice of the Christian Mission" bibliography
Tribes and castes of the North-Western provinces and Oudh by W. Crooke
"Virtue in the Ramayana : the relationship between Dharma, Karma, and Artha" by Dismore Alter
"Voices crying in the American wilderness," by David E. Swift
"Worshipping Congregation and Worldly Christianity" by Hans Dietrich Wendland
Various conversations - World Council of Churches Study Department
Conversations - includes David Scott, P. N. Chopra, and Robert Holmes
Conversation on communism and Christianity at Dr. Bates house
World Council of Churches study department
Lectures on theology to the natives in India
Christians in India: Issues and Activities. Topics include: Christian churches and the Indian government; Social issues and caste system; Issues of religious freedom; Rights of Christians in India
Religious Issues in India. Topics include: Catholic and Orthodox churches in India; Indian cultures and religions; Religious conversions
News article, "Assignment: The Subcontinent - India", published in Harrison News Herald, Cadiz, Ohio in 1975, by Milton and Nately Ronsheim, Presbyterian missionaries to India.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Folder labels in quotations are Alter's designations.
